message:
fix(websocket): prevent pong queue memory DoS and fix FIFO dispatch

- Cap pending pongs to 16 entries in both client and server WebSocket
  implementations to prevent memory exhaustion from Ping flooding attacks
  (RFC 6455 allows responding with just the latest payload)
- Replace inefficient reverse-then-pop pattern with remove(0) for true
  FIFO dispatch of pending pongs without reversing the whole queue
- Gracefully ignore close errors on cancellation in client send() to
  avoid masking the actual cancellation error with a close frame failure
- Remove redundant close initiation in recv() cancellation path since
  the caller handles cleanup

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
